methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Heirloom Carbon is a Series B climate and energy company that has raised $150M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 9 private peers (Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, CarbonCapture Inc., and Cemvita among them) and 1 public reference point, all operating within the Climate & Energy sector at or near Series B stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $150M total raised as a baseline and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, implying a valuation of $285M. Peer-median analysis across the 5-company same-stage, same-sector cohort yields a median valuation of $310M, with a range spanning $120M (Cemvita) to $500M (Pano AI). The peer set demonstrates meaningful dispersion, reflecting heterogeneity in technology maturity and commercial traction within the frontier climate tech segment.

The triangulated valuation range is $242M to $356M, with a median point of $298M. The stage-progression and peer-median approaches converge tightly around $285M–$310M, suggesting modest confidence in a $280M–$320M midpoint. Key uncertainties include the comparability of peer revenue multiples and technology risk profiles, which are not quantified in this dataset, as well as capital efficiency and deployment timelines specific to Heirloom's business model relative to peers.
